courses.title: Interview Skills Essentials
courses.description: Learn how to prepare for interviews, answer common questions, and follow up professionally.
courses.objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to describe the interview process, prepare targeted stories using the STAR method, and practice answering behavioral questions.
courses.outline:
Module 1: Understanding the interview process
Module 2: Researching employers
Module 3: Common interview questions
Module 4: Behavioral interview practice
Module 5: Following up after the interview

occupations.title: Administrative Assistant
occupations.description: Provide administrative support such as scheduling, correspondence, and data entry in an office setting.
occupations.typical_duties: Manage calendars, answer phones, prepare documents, organize files, and support office operations.
occupations.work_environment: Primarily office-based, working at a desk with computers and office equipment, often in a team setting.
skills_required: Proficiency with word processing and spreadsheets
occupations.education_requirements: High school diploma or equivalent; some employers prefer postsecondary training or an associate degree.

occupations.title: Customer Service Representative
occupations.description: Interact with customers to handle complaints, process orders, and provide information about products and services.
occupations.typical_duties: Answer customer inquiries by phone, email, or chat; resolve issues; document interactions; escalate complex problems.
occupations.work_environment: Call centers, retail settings, or office environments; may involve shift work and fast-paced interactions.
occupations.education_requirements: High school diploma or equivalent; on-the-job training is common.

occupations.title: IT Support Specialist
occupations.description: Provide technical assistance to computer users, resolving hardware and software problems.
occupations.typical_duties: Respond to help desk tickets, troubleshoot technical issues, install and configure software, and document solutions.
occupations.work_environment: Office environments, call centers, or remote support; frequent interaction with non-technical users.
skills_required: Basic networking and operating system knowledge
occupations.education_requirements: Postsecondary certificate or associate degree in information technology or related field is common.

occupations.title: Career Counselor
occupations.description: Help individuals explore careers, make education and training decisions, and develop job search strategies.
occupations.typical_duties: Conduct assessments, review resumes, coach on interview skills, and connect clients to resources.
occupations.work_environment: Nonprofit organizations, schools, colleges, workforce centers; combination of one-on-one and group sessions.
occupations.education_requirements: Master’s degree in counseling, psychology, or a related field is often required.

occupations.title: Registered Nurse
occupations.description: Provide and coordinate patient care, educate patients and the public about health conditions, and provide advice and emotional support.
occupations.typical_duties: Assess patient health, administer medications, collaborate with doctors, and maintain detailed records.
occupations.work_environment: Hospitals, clinics, long-term care facilities, and community settings; may require evenings, nights, and weekends.
occupations.education_requirements: Associate or bachelor’s degree in nursing and state licensure.

occupations.title: Medical Assistant
occupations.description: Perform administrative and certain clinical duties in healthcare settings.
occupations.typical_duties: Schedule appointments, take vital signs, prepare patients for exams, and maintain medical records.
occupations.work_environment: Physicians’ offices, clinics, and outpatient care centers; generally daytime hours.
occupations.education_requirements: Postsecondary certificate or diploma in medical assisting; some states require certification.

occupations.title: Receptionist
occupations.description: Answer phones, greet visitors, and perform basic administrative tasks.
occupations.typical_duties: Answer and route calls, greet guests, schedule appointments, and handle mail.
occupations.work_environment: Offices, clinics, and community organizations; front-desk or lobby area.
occupations.education_requirements: High school diploma or equivalent; short-term on-the-job training.

occupations.title: Office Manager
occupations.description: Oversee the administrative activities of an office, ensuring efficient operations.
occupations.typical_duties: Supervise administrative staff, manage budgets, coordinate office services, and improve processes.
occupations.work_environment: Office environments in many industries, often full-time weekday schedules.
occupations.education_requirements: High school diploma plus experience; some employers prefer an associate or bachelor’s degree.

occupations.title: Retail Sales Associate
occupations.description: Assist customers in retail stores, process sales, and maintain product displays.
occupations.typical_duties: Greet customers, answer questions, recommend products, and operate cash registers.
occupations.work_environment: Retail stores and shopping centers; may involve evenings, weekends, and holidays.
occupations.education_requirements: High school diploma or equivalent; on-the-job training is typical.

occupations.title: Training and Development Specialist
occupations.description: Plan, conduct, and administer programs that train employees and improve their skills and knowledge.
occupations.typical_duties: Assess training needs, develop materials, deliver training sessions, and evaluate effectiveness.
occupations.work_environment: Corporate offices, nonprofits, and government agencies; mix of office work and classroom training.
occupations.education_requirements: Bachelor’s degree in human resources, education, or related field is common.

resource_items.title: Career Journeys: From Retail to Tech Support
resource_items.description: Podcast episode featuring a career changer story.

resource_items.title: 30-Day Job Search Plan

resource_items.title: Using Informational Interviews to Explore Careers

resource_items.title: What to Expect in a Group Interview
resource_items.description: Strategies for standing out in group and panel interviews.

resource_items.title: Interview Question Generator
resource_items.description: Interactive tool that creates practice questions based on your target job.

resource_items.title: Do You Still Need a Cover Letter?
resource_items.description: When to send a cover letter and what to include.

training_programs.title: IT Support Fundamentals
training_programs.description: Entry-level training program covering core IT support skills for help desk roles.
training_programs.overview: This 10-week program introduces hardware, software, networking basics, and customer service for IT support.
training_programs.curriculum_highlights: PC hardware, operating systems, basic networking, ticketing systems, and troubleshooting.
training_programs.requirements: High school diploma or equivalent; basic computer familiarity.

training_programs.title: Healthcare Administration Basics
training_programs.description: Foundational program for administrative roles in clinics and hospitals.
training_programs.overview: Learn medical terminology, healthcare office procedures, and patient scheduling systems over 16 weeks.
training_programs.curriculum_highlights: Medical terminology, insurance basics, electronic health records, and customer service.
training_programs.requirements: High school diploma or equivalent; background check may be required for internships.

training_programs.title: IT Helpdesk Bootcamp
training_programs.description: Intensive bootcamp preparing learners for entry-level help desk and support roles.
training_programs.overview: Hands-on labs and job search support included over 12 weeks.
training_programs.curriculum_highlights: Operating systems, troubleshooting labs, soft skills for support, and certification prep.
training_programs.requirements: High school diploma; completion of basic computer skills assessment.

training_programs.title: Medical Assistant Training Program
training_programs.description: Comprehensive training for clinical and administrative medical assistant roles.
training_programs.overview: Classroom instruction combined with a clinical externship in local clinics.
training_programs.curriculum_highlights: Phlebotomy basics, vital signs, medical law and ethics, and front-office procedures.
training_programs.requirements: High school diploma or equivalent; immunizations and background check required.

training_programs.title: Office Administration Skills Certificate
training_programs.description: Prepare for administrative assistant and office support roles.
training_programs.overview: Develop Microsoft Office skills, business communication, and basic bookkeeping.
training_programs.curriculum_highlights: Word processing, spreadsheets, customer service, and office procedures.

training_programs.title: Business Essentials for Emerging Leaders
training_programs.description: Short program focusing on core business concepts and leadership skills.
training_programs.overview: Ideal for those moving into supervisory roles or considering business studies.
training_programs.curriculum_highlights: Management basics, communication, and project coordination.

training_programs.title: CNC Machining and Manufacturing Basics
training_programs.description: Hands-on training for entry-level CNC operator roles in manufacturing.
training_programs.overview: Learn safety, blueprint reading, and machine operation over 14 weeks.
training_programs.curriculum_highlights: Shop math, measurement tools, CNC setup, and quality control.
training_programs.requirements: High school diploma or equivalent; mechanical aptitude recommended.

training_programs.title: Healthcare IT Coordinator Training
training_programs.description: Cross-training for roles at the intersection of healthcare and IT.
training_programs.overview: Covers electronic health records systems, privacy regulations, and basic IT support.
training_programs.curriculum_highlights: EHR workflows, HIPAA, help desk skills, and change management.
training_programs.requirements: Experience in either healthcare or IT preferred.

training_programs.title: Evening IT Support Certificate
training_programs.description: Part-time evening program for working adults interested in IT support careers.
training_programs.overview: Classes held three evenings per week with optional Saturday labs.
training_programs.curriculum_highlights: Hardware, software, networking, and certification preparation.
training_programs.requirements: High school diploma; math placement test.

job_postings.title: Administrative Assistant - Community Nonprofit
job_postings.description: Provide front office and administrative support for a busy nonprofit offering employment and housing services. Duties include answering phones, scheduling appointments, maintaining client records, and data entry.

job_postings.title: Front Desk Administrative Assistant
job_postings.description: Entry-level administrative role supporting a community health clinic. Responsibilities include greeting patients, managing check-in, scheduling follow-up visits, and handling insurance paperwork.

job_postings.title: Office Administrative Assistant
job_postings.description: Support attorneys and staff with scheduling, document preparation, filing, and receptionist duties in a fast-paced nonprofit legal office.

job_postings.title: Administrative Assistant
job_postings.description: Handle general office tasks, respond to tenant inquiries, and coordinate maintenance requests.

job_postings.title: Senior Administrative Assistant
job_postings.description: Provide high-level administrative support to program directors, including complex calendar management and report preparation.

job_postings.title: Receptionist / Office Assistant
job_postings.description: Greet visitors, manage a multi-line phone system, order office supplies, and support light administrative tasks for a design studio.

job_postings.title: Customer Service Representative
job_postings.description: Assist customers in person and by phone, handle returns and exchanges, and maintain a clean store environment.

job_postings.title: Remote Customer Support Associate
job_postings.description: Provide phone and chat support to customers for a range of digital products. Training provided.

job_postings.title: IT Support Specialist I
job_postings.description: Handle Tier 1 support tickets, reset passwords, and assist staff with basic hardware and software issues in a nonprofit environment.

job_postings.title: Help Desk Technician
job_postings.description: Provide remote and on-site technical support for staff across multiple clinic locations.

job_postings.title: Medical Assistant Trainee
job_postings.description: Work alongside experienced medical assistants while completing a training program. Duties include rooming patients, taking vitals, and updating charts.

job_postings.title: Registered Nurse - Outpatient Clinic
job_postings.description: Provide nursing care in a busy outpatient clinic serving diverse patients. Daytime schedule with occasional evenings.

job_postings.title: Career Counselor
job_postings.description: Provide one-on-one and group career counseling to job seekers, including assessments, resume reviews, and interview coaching.

job_postings.title: Office Manager
job_postings.description: Oversee administrative operations for a small community services organization, supervise two administrative assistants, and coordinate facilities needs.

job_postings.title: Retail Sales Associate
job_postings.description: Support store operations by assisting customers, sorting donations, and operating the cash register in a nonprofit thrift store.

job_postings.title: Administrative Assistant
job_postings.description: Provide administrative and reception support in a senior living community. Not BART accessible.

events.description: Interactive resume workshop followed by a computer lab session where staff can help you revise your resume on the spot.

events.title: LinkedIn Profile Lab
events.description: Hands-on lab to set up or improve your LinkedIn profile, including headline, summary, and experience sections.

events.title: Group Mock Interview Practice
events.description: Practice common interview questions in a supportive small-group setting with feedback from a career coach.

events.title: Morning Resume Basics
events.description: Learn the essential parts of a resume, common mistakes to avoid, and how to tailor your resume to a job posting.

events.title: Youth Resume Lab
events.description: Drop-in lab for youth and young adults to create or revise their first resume with support from staff.

events.title: Polish Your Resume for Career Change
events.description: Focused session on updating your resume when changing careers, with examples and peer feedback.

events.title: Job Search Orientation
events.description: Overview of services, how to use the job board, and tips for organizing your job search.

events.title: Community Networking Mixer
events.description: Informal networking event with local employers, training providers, and job seekers.

events.title: Careers in Healthcare and IT Panel
events.description: Hear from professionals working in healthcare and information technology about their career paths and advice.

events.title: Interviewing Basics Webinar
events.description: Learn how to answer common interview questions, talk about your strengths, and follow up after an interview.

events.title: Employer Information Session: Community Tech Collaborative
events.description: Meet recruiters from Community Tech Collaborative and learn about their open IT support roles and hiring process.

mentors.name: Cameron Lee
mentors.headline: Senior IT Support Engineer at Community Tech Collaborative
mentors.bio: Cameron has over 8 years of experience in IT support, starting in a nonprofit help desk and moving into senior engineer roles. They enjoy helping career changers build practical skills and confidence for entry-level IT roles.

mentors.name: Alex Rivera
mentors.headline: Service Desk Manager in Healthcare IT
mentors.bio: Alex manages a service desk team supporting electronic health records systems. Former career changer from retail, Alex focuses on helping mentees translate past experience into IT opportunities.

mentors.name: Taylor Nguyen
mentors.headline: Junior Systems Administrator
mentors.bio: Taylor recently completed an IT training bootcamp and now works as a systems administrator. They mentor beginners on how to survive the first year in tech roles.

mentors.name: Jordan Clark
mentors.headline: IT Support Specialist II at City Workforce Center
mentors.bio: Jordan supports staff and public computer labs at a workforce center. They specialize in coaching job seekers on digital literacy and entry-level IT support pathways.

mentors.name: Maria Sanchez
mentors.headline: Registered Nurse and Clinical Preceptor
mentors.bio: Maria has worked in community health clinics for over a decade and supervises nursing students and new graduates.

mentors.name: Daniel Kim
mentors.headline: Program Manager at Workforce Nonprofit
mentors.bio: Daniel oversees employment programs and has a background in business operations and project management.

mentors.name: Riley Jones
mentors.headline: UX Designer at Civic Tech Startup
mentors.bio: Riley designs user-centered products for local government services and mentors aspiring designers from underrepresented communities.

mentors.name: Patricia Brown
mentors.headline: Career Coach and Workshop Facilitator
mentors.bio: Patricia has 12 years of experience helping job seekers clarify goals, build resumes, and prepare for interviews.

mentors.name: Neha Patel
mentors.headline: Medical Assistant Supervisor
mentors.bio: Neha supervises a team of medical assistants in a busy outpatient clinic and enjoys mentoring people entering healthcare for the first time.

mentors.name: Evan Wilson
mentors.headline: Small Business Owner and Former Office Manager
mentors.bio: Evan runs a small bookkeeping firm and previously worked as an office manager. They mentor clients interested in administrative and small business careers.
